info.title: Managed Deep Agents API
info.description: Create, configure, and run Managed Deep Agents.

tags.name: Agents
tags.description: Create and manage Managed Deep Agent resources.

tags.name: Threads
tags.description: Create and inspect durable thread state.

tags.name: Runs
tags.description: Start runs on Managed Deep Agent threads.

tags.name: MCP servers
tags.description: Register MCP servers and store credentials referenced by agent tools.

tags.name: MCP tools
tags.description: Discover tools exposed by registered MCP servers.

tags.name: Auth sessions
tags.description: Start and poll OAuth authorization sessions for OAuth MCP servers.

get.description: Return Managed Deep Agents owned by the authenticated user. System-created default agents are excluded.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Items per page. Defaults to 20. Maximum is 100.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Substring filter on agent name.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort by.

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort order.

post.description: Create an agent with metadata, runtime configuration, and an optional file tree. Creation is atomic: either the agent is fully created or no state is persisted.

value.name: research-assistant
value.description: Research assistant that can search the web and summarize sources.
value.instructions: You are a careful research assistant. Search for sources, keep notes, and return concise answers with citations.

value.name: research-assistant
value.description: Research assistant that can search the web and summarize sources.
value.instructions: You are a careful research assistant. Search for sources, keep notes, and return concise answers with citations.

get.description: Return the specified agent, including metadata, permissions, runtime, extras, and the parsed file tree at the latest commit. Pass `include_files=true` to include the raw file map.

patch.description: Update the specified agent. Top-level scalar fields merge field-by-field. Nested objects such as `runtime`, `permissions`, `tools`, `subagents`, `skills`, and `extras` are replaced in full when provided. Providing file-tree fields such as `instructions`, `tools`, `subagents`, `skills`, or `files` creates a new file tree commit.

value.description: Research assistant that searches the web, reads URLs, and summarizes sources.
value.instructions: You are a careful research assistant. Search for sources, read relevant pages, keep notes, and return concise answers with citations.

delete.description: Delete the agent. The call is idempotent: deleting a non-existent agent returns `204`. Deletion does not cascade to the agent's threads — existing threads remain queryable but cannot start new runs (attempts return `502`). Delete threads explicitly when you want to clean them up.

get.description: Return one page of recent threads for a Managed Deep Agent. Results are ordered by latest conversation activity.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Managed Deep Agent ID.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Items per page. Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by the previous list call.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter to a single thread status.

parameters.name: is_pinned
parameters.description: Filter by pinned state.

parameters.name: test_run
parameters.description: Return test-run threads instead of non-test threads. Defaults to `false`.

parameters.name: requires_attention
parameters.description: Return only threads that need user action. Cannot be combined with `status`.

post.description: Create a thread bound to a Managed Deep Agent. Use the returned thread ID to start runs.

basic.summary: Create a thread for an agent

post.description: Search threads. Request and response bodies are proxied unchanged to the upstream agent runtime.

200.description: Threads returned from the upstream runtime.

get.description: Return the caller inbox `attention_count`: unread idle threads plus interrupted and errored threads. Scoped to deep-agent threads; test-run threads are excluded.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Optional Managed Deep Agent ID to scope the count.

get.description: Return thread metadata and status for the given thread ID.

patch.description: Update thread fields such as title and metadata. Omitted fields are unchanged.

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: Thread ID.

delete.description: Delete a thread and all of its runs. Idempotent: deleting a missing thread returns 204.

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: Thread ID.

post.description: Start a run on the thread. This endpoint is proxied to the upstream agent runtime and accepts its run payload. Include the assistant or agent identifier and run inputs in the request body.
Start a run on a thread and stream output as server-sent events. The request must use `agent_id`; `assistant_id` is reserved for server-side forwarding and is rejected.

messages.content: Research recent approaches to agent memory and summarize the main tradeoffs.

post.description: Complete a human-interrupt pause on the thread without sending new input, allowing execution to continue or finish. On success the response has no body.

Register an MCP server in the caller's workspace. Static-header servers can include credential headers. OAuth servers should set `auth_type=oauth` and `oauth_mode=per_user_dynamic_client`, then register an OAuth provider and start an auth session before use.

basic.summary: Register Tavily's hosted MCP server with a bearer credential

get.description: List MCP servers registered in the caller's workspace.
Fetch a single MCP server by ID.

patch.description: Update an MCP server's URL, credential headers, or auth configuration. Passing `headers` replaces the entire stored header array — partial diffs are not supported. Use this endpoint to rotate credentials.

delete.description: Delete an MCP server. The call is idempotent: deleting a non-existent server returns `204`. After deletion, agents whose tools reference this server's URL will no longer have the stored headers attached at invocation time.

post.description: Discovers and registers an OAuth provider for the authenticated user against a Deep Agents MCP server configured with per-user dynamic client mode. Idempotent when a mapping already exists.

empty.summary: Infer the user from the authenticated request

get.description: Return tools exposed by a registered MCP server. The API serves cached results when fresh; otherwise, it fetches tools from the remote MCP server and caches the response.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Registered MCP server URL to fetch tools from.

parameters.name: oauth_provider_id
parameters.description: OAuth provider ID for token-based MCP server auth.

parameters.name: force_refresh
parameters.description: Skip cached tool definitions and fetch fresh tools from the MCP server.

parameters.name: ls_user_id
parameters.description: User ID override for service-key callers.

tools.name: read_url_content
tools.description: Read and extract content from a URL.

post.description: Start an OAuth authorization session for the caller. If the user is already authorized, the response can be completed immediately. Otherwise, the response includes a verification URL that the user must visit to complete authorization.

oauth.summary: Start an OAuth session for an MCP server provider

200.description: Existing token reused; no new session created.

201.description: New pending session created; visit `verification_url` to complete authorization.

get.description: Return the current status of an in-flight authorization session. Use `wait_seconds` to long-poll until the session completes or the wait window expires.

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Authorization session ID.

parameters.name: wait_seconds
parameters.description: Long-poll window in seconds. Defaults to 1. Maximum is 25.

post.description: Create a new agent that mirrors the source agent behavior but is owned by the caller. The clone copies runtime, backend, file tree, tools, subagents, and skills; caller metadata and sharing state start fresh.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Managed Deep Agent ID.

parameters.name: include_files
parameters.description: Include the raw file map in the agent response. Accepted truthy values are `true` and `1`.

parameters.name: X-User-Timezone
parameters.description: IANA timezone name (for example, `America/Los_Angeles`) stamped into the agent's runtime configuration when the agent is created or updated. The runtime uses this timezone to inject a localized current-date line into the agent's system prompt, so the agent reasons about dates in the user's local time. Optional. Defaults to `UTC` when omitted; invalid values fall back to `UTC`.

get.description: Return a per-MCP-server health summary for the agent.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Managed Deep Agent ID.

post.summary: Create a thread and start a run
post.description: Create a thread bound to `agent_id` and start a run on it in one call. Run kwargs are passed through; thread-creation flags go in the optional `thread` envelope.

metadata.title: Create a thread and start a run

post.description: Apply one thread update payload to multiple threads. Returns one result per thread in request order.

IncludeFiles.name: include_files
IncludeFiles.description: Include the raw file map in the agent response. Accepted truthy values are `true` and `1`.

UserTimezone.name: X-User-Timezone
UserTimezone.description: IANA timezone name (for example, `America/Los_Angeles`) stamped into the agent's runtime configuration when the agent is created or updated. The runtime uses this timezone to inject a localized current-date line into the agent's system prompt, so the agent reasons about dates in the user's local time. Optional. Defaults to `UTC` when omitted; invalid values fall back to `UTC`.

value.detail: Set 'name' to a non-empty string: 'name' is required
Provide a valid JSON request body matching the documented schema

Unauthorized.description: Authentication required. The auth layer returns a simple `{"error": "Unauthorized"}` body that does not match the structured `ErrorResponse` shape used by other 4xx responses.

Forbidden.description: The authenticated identity does not have access to the resource or feature. The auth layer returns a simple `{"error": "Forbidden"}` body that does not match the structured `ErrorResponse` shape used by other 4xx responses.

invalidApiKey.summary: API key is invalid or lacks workspace access

value.detail: Verify the agent ID and that the calling identity has access: agent not found

Conflict.description: Request conflicts with current resource state.

UnprocessableEntity.description: Request is syntactically valid but failed validation.

upstreamAuthFailed.summary: Upstream runtime rejected the request (e.g. orphaned thread after agent deletion)

value.detail: Verify that the calling identity is authorized for this organization: upstream agent runtime rejected the request

name.description: Human-readable agent name. Must not be blank.

instructions.description: Deprecated alias for `system_prompt`. Accepted for backwards compatibility; `system_prompt` takes precedence when both are set.

subagents.description: Subagents. Each entry is written to `subagents/<name>/AGENTS.md` and, when provided, `subagents/<name>/tools.json`.

skills.description: Inline skills. Each entry is written to `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` and supporting files.

extras.description: Caller-defined key-value metadata attached to the agent.

system_prompt.description: Agent system prompt. Written to `AGENTS.md`. Preferred over deprecated `instructions` when both are provided.

configurable.description: Caller-defined runtime configuration merged into the agent config. Typed runtime fields win on key collision.

UpdateAgentRequest.description: Sparse update payload. Omitted fields are left unchanged.

instructions.description: Deprecated alias for `system_prompt`. Accepted for backwards compatibility; `system_prompt` takes precedence when both are set.

subagents.description: Updated subagent definitions. Replaces the existing subagents field in full.

skills.description: Updated inline skills. Replaces the existing skills field in full.

extras.description: Updated caller-defined metadata. Replaces the existing extras field in full.

system_prompt.description: Updated agent system prompt. Written to `AGENTS.md`. Preferred over deprecated `instructions` when both are provided.

configurable.description: Updated caller-defined runtime configuration. Typed runtime fields win on key collision.

deleted_paths.description: Relative file paths to remove from the file tree.

AgentPermissions.description: Agent visibility and sharing configuration. When omitted on create, defaults to personal identity, tenant visibility, and read tenant access.

read.description: User IDs allowed to read the agent.

run.description: User IDs allowed to run the agent.

update.description: User IDs allowed to update the agent.

model_id.description: Model identifier used to run the agent. Use the form `{provider}:{model_name}` — for example, `openai:gpt-5.5` or `openai:gpt-5.4-mini`. Values without a colon are interpreted as references to a saved Playground configuration, not as direct model identifiers. See [Supported models](/langsmith/managed-deep-agents-overview#supported-models) for the recognized provider prefixes.

tools.description: MCP tools available to the agent. Serialized to `tools.json`.

interrupt_config.description: Per-tool interrupt rules. Keys are tool identifiers in the form `{mcp_server_url}::{tool_name}` (separator is two colons). The URL is normalized with any trailing slash stripped, so `https://tools.example.com` and `https://tools.example.com/` match the same tool. Additional `::`-separated components after the tool name (such as the MCP server display name) are accepted but ignored when matching. A bare tool name with no `::` is also accepted as a fallback. Values are booleans: `true` requires a human-in-the-loop interrupt before the tool runs; `false` allows it without interrupt. Use `{}` for no interrupts.

name.description: Tool name as exposed by the MCP server.

mcp_server_url.description: URL of the MCP server hosting this tool.

mcp_server_name.description: Display name for the MCP server. Defaults to the URL when empty.

display_name.description: Display name for the tool. Defaults to the tool name when empty.

name.description: Subagent slug. Must be non-blank and may contain letters, numbers, spaces, underscores, or hyphens.

model_id.description: Model ID for this subagent. Inherits the parent runtime when empty.

name.description: Skill slug. Must be non-blank and may contain letters, numbers, spaces, underscores, or hyphens.

description.description: Short human-readable description shown to the agent.

files.description: Supporting files keyed by relative path. Paths must be relative with no leading slash or `..` segment.

FileMap.description: Raw file map for paths not covered by typed fields. Keys are relative file paths. Setting a typed field and the corresponding `files` entry returns `422`.

type.description: Entry discriminator. Defaults to `file` when omitted.

content.description: File contents as a UTF-8 string. Must be empty when `type` is `skill`.
content.example:
# AGENTS.md

You are a helpful agent.

repo_handle.description: Workspace skill repo handle. Required when `type` is `skill`.

commit_id.description: Optional pin to a specific skill commit. Defaults to latest.

access_level.description: Caller access level for this agent.

is_owner.description: Whether the authenticated caller owns this agent.

revision.description: Revision token for the latest file tree commit.

instructions.description: Deprecated alias echoed alongside `system_prompt` for backwards compatibility.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for the next page, or `null` when there are no more pages.

test_run.description: Marks the thread as a test run, filtered out of usage and analytics.

skip_memory_write_protection.description: Controls whether the runtime pauses for human approval before the agent writes to long-term memory on this thread. By default (`false`), the runtime raises a human-in-the-loop interrupt when the agent tries to write or edit files in its memory directory, so the user can approve or reject the write before it persists. Set to `true` to bypass that interrupt and let memory writes proceed immediately — useful for headless or unattended runs.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for the next page, or `null` when there are no more pages.

agent_id.description: Managed Deep Agent ID. The server forwards this upstream as `assistant_id`.

input.description: Run input state. Include `messages` for chat input; additional properties are forwarded as graph input state.

user_timezone.description: IANA timezone name (for example, `America/Los_Angeles`) for this run. The runtime uses it to inject a localized current-date line into the agent's system prompt, so the agent reasons about dates in the user's local time. Optional. Defaults to the timezone stamped on the agent (or `UTC` if none was set); invalid values fall back to `UTC`.

PassThroughJSON.description: JSON payload passed through to the upstream agent runtime.

McpServerHeader.description: A single header attached to MCP server invocations.

value.description: Header value. May contain secrets such as bearer tokens.

name.description: Human-readable name for the MCP server.

url.description: MCP server URL. Must be a valid HTTPS endpoint.

headers.description: Credential headers attached to every invocation of this MCP server. Omitted from response bodies when the caller lacks invoke permission on the server.

oauth_provider_id.description: Optional OAuth provider ID for legacy shared OAuth providers.

vendor_id.description: Optional vendor identifier for catalog grouping.

UpdateMcpServerRequest.description: All fields are optional. Passing `headers` replaces the entire stored header array.

headers.description: Credential headers attached to every invocation of this MCP server. Omitted from response bodies when the caller lacks invoke permission on the server. Passing this field replaces the entire stored array.

oauth_provider_id.description: Optional OAuth provider ID for legacy shared OAuth providers.

headers.description: Credential headers attached to every invocation of this MCP server. Omitted from response bodies when the caller lacks invoke permission on the server.

oauth_provider_id.description: OAuth provider ID for legacy shared OAuth providers, when configured.

can_invoke.description: Whether the caller has invoke permission on this server. Omitted when ambient permissions already imply invoke.

ListMcpServersResponse.description: List of MCP servers in the caller's workspace.

tools.description: Tools exposed by the MCP server.

cached.description: Whether the response came from the MCP tools cache.

fetched_at.description: UTC timestamp when the tools were fetched from the MCP server.

name.description: Tool name as exposed by the MCP server.

inputSchema.description: JSON schema for the tool input.

annotations.description: MCP tool annotations returned by the server.

AuthType.description: Authentication scheme used by the MCP server.

OAuthMode.description: OAuth mode used by an OAuth MCP server.

AuthSessionStrategy.description: `REUSE` returns an existing valid token when possible. `CREATE` starts a fresh authorization session.

provider_id.description: OAuth provider ID returned by the MCP OAuth provider registration endpoint.

agent_id.description: Optional agent ID to associate with the session.

token_id.description: Optional token ID to reuse or update.

is_default.description: Whether the resulting token should be the default token for this provider.

id.description: Authorization session ID. Present for pending sessions.

verification_url.description: URL the user must visit to complete authorization. Present when status is `PENDING`.

token.description: Token identifier or token payload returned when authorization is already complete. Treat this value as sensitive.

ls_user_id.description: Deprecated. User identity is inferred from the authenticated request when available.

oauth_provider_id.description: OAuth provider ID to use when starting an authorization session.

scope.description: Sandbox scope. Use `thread` for one sandbox per thread, or `agent` for one sandbox shared by the agent.

snapshot_id.description: Snapshot used when creating new sandbox-backed computers.

idle_ttl_seconds.description: Idle timeout after which an unused sandbox is paused, in seconds.

delete_after_stop_seconds.description: Time after a sandbox is stopped before it is deleted, in seconds.

type.description: Execution backend type. Use `state` for state-only agents or `sandbox` for sandbox-backed agents.

attention_count.description: Number of threads needing user action.

HealthResponse.description: Per-MCP-server health summary for an agent.
